stimulus: The wooden barrels used to ship grain across the American frontier in the mid-1800s were heavy and difficult to seal. ______blank because they were the only large containers strong enough to survive long wagon journeys, these barrels remained the standard. That all changed in 1879, when inventor Robert Gair developed a machine to mass-produce sturdy, lightweight cardboard boxes.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. "However" logically signals that the information in this sentence—that wooden barrels remained the standard—contrasts with the previous claim that these barrels were heavy and difficult to seal.

Choice A is incorrect because "for instance" illogically signals exemplification. Choice B is incorrect because "thus" illogically signals consequence; the second sentence does not result from the first. Choice D "In other words" illogically signals a restatement.
